chunks.title: Components & Dealing
chunks.content:
## Components & Dealing

**Components:** 58 cards, 6 game aids, 1 rulebook.

### Setup Steps
1. Shuffle all 58 cards into a face-down central draw pile.
2. Flip the top 2 cards face-up to form **two separate discard piles**.
3. Deal **3 cards** to each player as their starting hand.

### No Per-Player Boards or Tokens
No starting resources beyond the 3 dealt cards. Game aids are reference cards only.

chunks.title: Points Target by Player Count
chunks.content:
## Points Target by Player Count

The game ends when any player hits the target after a completed round.

| Players | Points to End Game |
|---------|-------------------|
| 2       | 40 pts            |
| 3       | 35 pts            |
| 4       | 30 pts            |

All other setup steps (shuffle, 2 discard piles, 3-card deal) are identical regardless of player count.

chunks.title: Turn Actions
chunks.content:
## Turn Structure

Each turn has two steps: **1) Draw** (mandatory), then **2) Play Duos** (optional).

### Step 1 — Add a Card to Your Hand (choose one)

| Option | Action |
|--------|--------|
| **A — Draw from Deck** | Take top 2 cards from the draw pile; keep 1, place the other face-up on either discard pile |
| **B — Take from Discard** | Take the top card from either discard pile and add it to your hand |

### Step 2 — Play Duo Cards (optional)

If you hold a matching duo pair, place them face-up in front of you, score **1 point per pair**, and apply their effect. You may trigger multiple duo effects in one turn.

| Duo Pair | Effect |
|----------|--------|
| Fish + Fish | Draw the top card of the deck into your hand |
| Boat + Boat | Take another full turn immediately |
| Crab + Crab | Search one discard pile and take any card from it |
| Crab + Lobster | Take one card from **each** discard pile |
| Swimmer + Shark | Steal a random card from any one opponent's hand |

chunks.title: Ending a Round — STOP vs LAST CHANCE
chunks.content:
## Ending a Round

You may only call an end to the round if your current score (hand + played tableau) is **7 points or more**.

| Call | What Happens |
|------|--------------|
| **STOP** | Round ends immediately. **All players** (including caller) score their card points + color bonus. |
| **LAST CHANCE** | Every other player gets one more turn, then scoring resolves (see below). |

### LAST CHANCE Scoring

| Result | Caller Scores | All Other Players Score |
|--------|--------------|------------------------|
| Caller has highest (or tied highest) card score | Card points + color bonus | Color bonus only |
| Any other player beats the caller's card score | Color bonus only | Card points + color bonus |

chunks.title: Duo Card Effects
chunks.content:
## Duo Card Effects

Playing a matched pair activates an effect **and** scores **1 pt per pair** at end of round.

| Pair | Effect |
|---|---|
| Fish + Fish | Draw the top card of the draw deck into your hand |
| Boat + Boat | Take another full turn immediately |
| Crab + Crab | Search **one** discard pile and take any card from it |
| Crab + Lobster | Take **one card from each** discard pile |
| Swimmer + Shark | Steal a **random** card from any one opponent's hand |

chunks.title: Collector & Bonus Card Scoring
chunks.content:
## Collector Card Scoring

All scored at **end of round** based on cards in your hand and played tableau.

| Card | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Shell | 0 | 2 | 4 | 6 | 8 | 10 |
| Octopus | 0 | 3 | 6 | 9 | 12 | — |
| Penguin | 1 | 3 | 5 | — | — | — |
| Sailor | 0 | 5 | — | — | — | — |
| Crab / Boat / Fish / Swimmer+Shark | 1 pt per matched pair | | | | | |

## Bonus / Multiplier Cards

| Card | Scoring Rule |
|---|---|
| Lighthouse | +1 pt per **Boat** card you have |
| Shoal of Fish | +1 pt per **Fish** card you have |

## Mermaid Cards

| Count | Effect |
|---|---|
| 1 Mermaid | +1 pt per card of your **most plentiful color** |
| 2 Mermaids | Also +1 pt per card of your **second most plentiful color** |
| All 4 Mermaids | **Instant game win** — round ends immediately, you win outright |

> **Color Bonus (all players):** At end of each round, every player scores +1 pt per card (hand + tableau) of their most common color. With 2 Mermaids, also score your second most common color.

chunks.title: End-of-Round Scoring — All VP Sources
chunks.content:
## End-of-Round Scoring

Add all of the following each round:

### Collector Cards
| Card | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 |
|------|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Shell | 0 | 2 | 4 | 6 | 8 | 10 |
| Octopus | 0 | 3 | 6 | 9 | 12 | — |
| Penguin | 1 | 3 | 5 | — | — | — |
| Sailor | 0 | 5 | — | — | — | — |

### Multiplier / Bonus Cards
- **Lighthouse:** +1 pt per Boat card you have
- **Shoal of Fish:** +1 pt per Fish card you have

### Mermaid Cards
- Each Mermaid: +1 pt per card of your **most plentiful color group**
- With 2 Mermaids: also +1 pt per card of your **second most common color**

### Duo Pairs
- Each played duo pair = **1 pt**

### Color Bonus (hand + tableau combined)
- +1 pt per card of your most common color
- With 2 Mermaids: also +1 pt per card of second most common color

---

## Game End
- Trigger: any player reaches **40 pts** (2p) / **35 pts** (3p) / **30 pts** (4p)
- **Most total points wins**
- **Tiebreaker:** player who did NOT call Stop/Last Chance wins the tie
- **Instant win:** collect all 4 Mermaid cards at any point → game over, you win

chunks.title: STOP vs LAST CHANCE — Scoring Consequences
chunks.content:
## STOP vs LAST CHANCE

When you end the round, choose one:

### STOP
- Round ends **immediately**
- **All players** score full card points + color bonus

### LAST CHANCE
- Every **other** player gets one more turn, then scores are compared:

| Outcome | Caller scores | Opponents score |
|---|---|---|
| Caller has highest card score (or tied) | Card points + color bonus | Color bonus only |
| Any opponent beats caller's card score | Color bonus only | Card points normally |

> **Risk/reward:** LAST CHANCE can shut out opponents, but backfires if someone out-scores you.

chunks.title: Sea Salt & Paper — Strategic Tips
chunks.content:
## Strategic Tips for First-Time Players

### Building Your Hand
- **Prioritize one color early.** The color bonus rewards your *most common* color, so diluting across many colors weakens your score every round — commit to a dominant color as soon as possible.
- **Shells are your backbone.** The shell set scales to 10 pts for 6 cards — the highest single-set ceiling in the game. Quietly accumulating shells while opponents watch Mermaids is a reliable path to victory.
- **Duo pairs are free value.** Playing a pair costs you nothing from your score — pairs score 1 pt *and* trigger an effect. Never sit on an unplayed pair when the effect is useful.

### Timing the Round End
- **Don't be the first to say STOP unless your lead is decisive.** LAST CHANCE is almost always the stronger call — opponents need to *beat* your score (not just match it) for your penalty to apply, and one extra turn rarely flips a solid lead.
- **Watch opponent hand sizes, not just your own score.** A player drawing aggressively and playing no duos is likely hoarding a big reveal — consider ending the round before they hit threshold.
- **The 7-pt visible threshold is a floor, not a target.** Wait until you have a meaningful cushion (10+ pts) before ending unless you need to deny an opponent's combo from maturing.

### Combos & Denial
- **Boat duo = double turn, use it to chain pairs.** If you have a Boat pair and another playable pair in hand, play Boat first — your second turn lets you play the second pair immediately for compounding effects.
- **Swimmer + Shark is a tempo weapon, not just theft.** Stealing into an opponent's hand right before a round-end call can both deny their score and push your own over the threshold to call STOP or LAST CHANCE profitably.
- **Track the 4-Mermaid win condition.** If any player has 2 Mermaids visible, treat the remaining Mermaid cards as hot commodities — either race to them or pivot to ending the round before the instant-win is reachable.
